mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and strong consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate profitability and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and an extremely low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an overheating economy with overly accommodative monetary policy, which typically precedes central bank tightening and subsequent negative equity market reactions as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are consistently positive when CPIAUCSL growth is high and FEDFUNDS is low, or the Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Robust and accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) during an economic expansion reflect strong consumer confidence and spending, which directly drives corporate revenue growth and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances during an expansionary regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals healthy manufacturing activity and overall economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a highly accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital, encourages investment, and supports asset valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
regime_hint: zero lower bound or near-zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a broad-based economic slowdown or recession, indicating reduced investment and consumer confidence, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average declines by > 10% AND UNRATE rises by > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where both HOUST declines and UNRATE rises significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) during a post-recession recovery period indicates a robust rebound in overall economic activity, leading to increased corporate profitability and strong S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of strong GDP growth in a post-recession recovery regime.
